name: LAX-101
description: LAX-101 treats hungtington’s disease, not just the symptoms but the disease itself. The active molecule in LAX-101 is Eicosapentaenoic Acid (EPA). All molecules of EPA are identical. The molecules of EPA found in LAX-101 are identical to the molecules of EPA found in fish oil food supplements.
indication: Investigated for use/treatment in depression and huntington's disease.
pharmacodynamics: LAX-101, a novel and proprietary compound that inhibits certain harmful enzymes including phospholipases and caspases, represents a new class of drugs sometimes referred to as NPLs. This class may function as "neuroprotectants" and appears to inhibit degradation of brain tissue by a variety of proposed mechanisms, including stabilization of the phospholipid components of cell membranes and mitochondria, cell structures that are important in cell regulation and brain function.
